annotations.label: Cubic equation

anchor.snippet: a cubic equation in one variable is an equation of the form

annotations.note: The `Cubic` structure with `toPoly = C a * X^3 + C b * X^2 + C c * X + C d` directly formalizes a cubic polynomial.

annotations.label: Real cubic has a real root

anchor.snippet: then it has at least one real root

annotations.note: The abstract fact that odd-degree polynomials over a real closed field have a root is stated, but no `IsRealClosed ℝ` instance is provided and there is no direct lemma for cubics over ℝ.

annotations.label: Reducible cubic with integer coefficients

anchor.snippet: is said to be reducible if the polynomial on the left-hand side is the product of polynomials of lower degrees

annotations.note: Generic `Irreducible`/reducibility of polynomials exists, but there is no cubic-specific reducibility definition over ℤ.

annotations.label: Rational root test for cubics

anchor.snippet: The rational root test allows finding q and p by examining a finite number of cases

annotations.note: The general rational root theorem (`num_dvd_of_is_root` and `den_dvd_of_is_root`) covers the cubic case.

annotations.label: Other roots via quadratic formula

anchor.snippet: the other roots are the roots of this quadratic polynomial and can be found by using the quadratic formula

annotations.note: The quadratic formula `quadratic_eq_zero_iff` is formalized, but its application to the quotient of a cubic by a known root is not packaged.

annotations.label: Depressed cubic

anchor.snippet: Cubics of the form

annotations.note: No specific `depressed cubic` definition (b=0 Cubic) is named in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reduction to depressed cubic via change of variable

anchor.snippet: The change of variable

annotations.note: The Tschirnhaus substitution x = t − b/(3a) for cubics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Roots relation between original and depressed cubic

anchor.snippet: The roots

annotations.note: The root correspondence under the depression substitution is not formalized.

annotations.label: Discriminant of a polynomial

anchor.snippet: The discriminant of a polynomial is a function of its coefficients that is zero if and only if the polynomial has a multiple root

annotations.note: Mathlib has degree-specific discriminants (`discrim` for quadratics, `Cubic.discr` for cubics) but no general `Polynomial.discriminant` definition.

annotations.label: Discriminant in terms of roots

anchor.snippet: are the three roots (not necessarily distinct nor real ) of the cubic

annotations.note: `Cubic.discr_eq_prod_three_roots` expresses the discriminant as `(a²(x−y)(x−z)(y−z))²` in a splitting field.

annotations.label: Discriminant of depressed cubic

anchor.snippet: The discriminant of the depressed cubic

annotations.note: `Cubic.discr` specializes to `−4p³−27q²` when `a=1, b=0`, but the depressed-cubic formula is not stated as a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Discriminant of general cubic

anchor.snippet: The discriminant of the general cubic

annotations.note: `Cubic.discr` is defined as `b²c² − 4ac³ − 4b³d − 27a²d² + 18abcd`, the standard cubic discriminant.

annotations.label: Equivalence and sign of two discriminants

anchor.snippet: one of these two discriminants is zero if and only if the other is also zero

annotations.note: The relation between the depressed-cubic and general-cubic discriminants is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nature of real cubic roots by discriminant sign

anchor.snippet: there are two cases

annotations.note: The classification of real cubic roots by sign of the discriminant (three real vs one real + conjugate pair) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex conjugate roots of real polynomial

anchor.snippet: if r is a root of a polynomial with real coefficients, then its complex conjugate is also a root

annotations.note: `Polynomial.aeval_conj` gives `aeval (conj z) p = conj (aeval z p)` for real `p`, so `p(z)=0` implies `p(conj z)=0`.

annotations.label: Zero discriminant implies multiple root

anchor.snippet: If the discriminant of a cubic is zero, the cubic has a multiple root

annotations.note: `Cubic.discr_ne_zero_iff_roots_nodup` characterizes nonzero discriminant by no duplicate roots, contrapositive of the stated fact.

annotations.label: Triple root case of depressed cubic

anchor.snippet: If p is also zero, then p = q = 0 , and 0 is a triple root of the cubic

annotations.note: The triple-root analysis for the depressed cubic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simple and double roots of depressed cubic with zero discriminant

anchor.snippet: the cubic has a simple root

annotations.note: Explicit simple/double root formulas in the zero-discriminant case are not formalized.

annotations.label: General cubic with zero discriminant

anchor.snippet: these results can be extended to the general cubic

annotations.note: Multiplicity characterization by discriminant zero exists, but explicit double/single root formulas for the general cubic are not.

annotations.label: Multiple root via common root with derivative

anchor.snippet: a multiple root is a common root of the polynomial and its formal derivative

annotations.note: `one_lt_rootMultiplicity_iff_isRoot` states that multiplicity > 1 iff `t` is a root of both `p` and `derivative p`.

annotations.label: Cardano's formula for depressed cubic

anchor.snippet: Cardano's result is that if

annotations.note: Cardano's radical formula for the depressed cubic is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Other two roots via primitive cube roots of unity

anchor.snippet: the two other roots are obtained by multiplying one of the cube roots by the primitive cube root of unity

annotations.note: Cube-root-of-unity rotation of Cardano roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Casus irreducibilis

anchor.snippet: This case has thus been called

annotations.note: The notion of casus irreducibilis is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cardano's formula via product condition

anchor.snippet: the product of the two cube roots must be

annotations.note: The uv = −p/3 product condition in Cardano's method is not formalized.

annotations.label: General cubic formula

anchor.snippet: A cubic formula for the roots of the general cubic equation

annotations.note: The closed-form radical formula for the general cubic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Three roots of general cubic via C and primitive cube root of unity

anchor.snippet: the three roots are

annotations.note: The C-and-ξ parametrization of cubic roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Viète's trigonometric formula for three real roots

anchor.section: Trigonometric solution for three real roots
anchor.snippet: the roots of the depressed cubic

annotations.note: Viète's trigonometric formula for the three real roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic formula for one real root

anchor.section: Hyperbolic solution for one real root
anchor.snippet: this root can be similarly represented using hyperbolic functions

annotations.note: The hyperbolic-function expression for the single real root is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chebyshev cube root

anchor.section: Hyperbolic solution for one real root
anchor.snippet: the above values of t 0 are sometimes called the Chebyshev cube root

annotations.note: Chebyshev cube root is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Omar Khayyám's geometric solution

anchor.snippet: Omar Khayyám constructed the parabola

annotations.note: Khayyám's parabola-circle intersection construction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Solvability with angle trisector

anchor.snippet: A cubic equation with real coefficients can be solved geometrically using compass, straightedge , and an angle trisector if and only if it has three real roots

annotations.note: Constructibility with trisector for cubics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compass-and-straightedge solvability

anchor.snippet: A cubic equation can be solved by compass-and-straightedge construction (without trisector) if and only if it has a rational root

annotations.note: Compass-and-straightedge solvability criterion for cubics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geometric interpretation of Viète's roots on circle

anchor.snippet: Viète's trigonometric expression of the roots in the three-real-roots case lends itself to a geometric interpretation in terms of a circle

annotations.note: The circle-based geometric interpretation of Viète's roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geometric meaning of real and complex roots in Cartesian plane

anchor.snippet: if there is only one real root, it is the abscissa

annotations.note: The Cartesian-plane geometric interpretation of cubic roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Marden's theorem for cubic roots

anchor.snippet: Marden's theorem says that the points representing the roots of the derivative of the cubic are the foci of the Steiner inellipse of the triangle

annotations.note: Marden's theorem and the Steiner inellipse are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Galois group of irreducible cubic is S3 or A3

anchor.snippet: this group is either the group S 3 of all six permutations of the three roots, or the group A 3 of the three circular permutations

annotations.note: `Polynomial.Gal` and `galActionHom` exist generally, but the cubic-specific S₃/A₃ dichotomy is not stated.

annotations.label: Galois group A3 iff discriminant is a square

anchor.snippet: the Galois group is A 3 if and only if the discriminant is the square of an element of K

annotations.note: The discriminant-square criterion for A₃ Galois group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cubic with Galois group A3

anchor.snippet: An example of a Galois group A 3 with three elements is given by

annotations.note: The specific A₃ Galois cubic example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cardano's method derivation

anchor.snippet: The idea is to introduce two variables u and

annotations.note: The u+v derivation of Cardano's formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vieta's substitution

anchor.snippet: Vieta's substitution is a method introduced by François Viète

annotations.note: Viète's t = w − p/(3w) substitution for cubics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lagrange's method via discrete Fourier transform

anchor.snippet: Lagrange's main idea was to work with the discrete Fourier transform of the roots instead of with the roots themselves

annotations.note: Lagrange's resolvent / DFT-of-roots derivation for cubics is not formalized.

annotations.label: S and P are symmetric functions

anchor.snippet: This shows that P and S are symmetric functions of the roots

annotations.note: Symmetric polynomials in roots and Vieta's formulas are formalized generally, but the specific Lagrange S and P symmetry argument for cubics is not.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues of 3×3 matrix as cubic roots

anchor.snippet: The eigenvalues of a 3×3 matrix are the roots of a cubic polynomial

annotations.note: The characteristic polynomial of a matrix is defined generally; the 3×3 case is implicit but no cubic-specific lemma exists.

annotations.label: Resolvent cubic of quartic

anchor.snippet: The solution of the general quartic equation relies on the solution of its resolvent cubic

annotations.note: The resolvent cubic and quartic solution are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Critical points of quartic

anchor.snippet: Critical points of a quartic function are found by solving a cubic equation

annotations.note: Critical points of a quartic via its cubic derivative are not formalized.

annotations.label: Inflection points of quintic

anchor.snippet: Inflection points of a quintic function are the solution of a cubic equation

annotations.note: Inflection points of a quintic via its cubic second derivative are not formalized.

annotations.label: Van der Waals equation cubic in volume

anchor.snippet: the Van der Waals equation of state , are cubic in the volume

annotations.note: Van der Waals equation is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler's third law cubic in semi-major axis

anchor.snippet: Kepler's third law of planetary motion is cubic in the semi-major axis

annotations.note: Kepler's third law is not formalized in Mathlib.
